steps.title: Step 4
steps.content: The product is $56y^{2}z^{2}$. The exponents are 2 because each variable appears once in each original factor. A is correct.

choices.explanation: • This would require only one z factor, but both (8yz) and (7z) contribute a z, giving z². Eliminate B.
• This is missing one y and one z exponent. Eliminate C.
• 8 + 7 = 15, not 16, and even addition is the wrong operation — coefficients multiply. Eliminate D.
